name: X: Search Posts
summary: Search X for posts from one handle, optionally within a date window, across top/latest/live tabs.
description: Search X for posts from one handle, optionally constrained to a date window, and return candidate post cards. Use when you want posts from a single author either recent (latest/top) or within a specific interval. Do not use to read one post or expand a thread (use `x_open` for both).

max_posts.description: Upper bound on extracted posts. Defaults to 40.

since_date.description: Inclusive lower-bound date in YYYY-MM-DD form (X 'since:' operator). Omit for no lower bound.

timeline_mode.description: Which X search tab to land on (defaults to latest).

until_date.description: Exclusive upper-bound date in YYYY-MM-DD form (X 'until:' operator). Omit for no upper bound.

steps.name: Open x.com in a fresh tab so direct search navigation is cookie-safe

inputs.script: const cleanArg = (v) => { const t = String(v == null ? '' : v).trim(); return /^\{[a-zA-Z0-9_]+\}$/.test(t) ? '' : t; }; const handle = cleanArg('{handle}').replace(/^@+/, ''); const since = cleanArg('{since_date}'); const until = cleanArg('{until_date}'); const rawMode = cleanArg('{timeline_mode}').toLowerCase(); const mode = ['top','latest','live'].includes(rawMode) ? rawMode : 'latest'; const modeFlag = mode === 'top' ? 'top' : (mode === 'live' ? 'live' : 'live'); const parts = ['from:' + handle]; if (since) parts.push('since:' + since); if (until) parts.push('until:' + until); const q = parts.join(' '); const url = 'https://x.com/search?q=' + encodeURIComponent(q) + '&src=typed_query&f=' + modeFlag; setTimeout(() => window.location.assign(url), 0); return { handle, since, until, mode, search_url: url };

steps.name: Build the search URL and navigate to it

target.selector: main article[data-testid='tweet'], main [data-testid='cellInnerDiv'] article, main article

inputs.target_selector: main article[data-testid='tweet'], main [data-testid='cellInnerDiv'] article, main article

steps.name: Scroll until enough candidate posts are visible

inputs.item_selector: main article[data-testid='tweet'], main [data-testid='cellInnerDiv'] article, main article

help.summary: Search X for posts from one handle, optionally within a date window, across top/latest/live tabs.

parameters.max_posts: Upper bound on extracted posts. Defaults to 40, max 100.
parameters.since_date: Inclusive lower-bound date in YYYY-MM-DD form (X 'since:' operator). Omit for no lower bound.
parameters.timeline_mode: Which X search tab to land on: top | latest | live. Defaults to latest.
parameters.until_date: Exclusive upper-bound date in YYYY-MM-DD form (X 'until:' operator). Omit for no upper bound.

examples.description: Most-recent posts from @elonmusk with no date window.

examples.command: rzn-browser run x search-posts --param handle="felixrieseberg" --param since_date="2026-03-10" --param until_date="2026-03-18" --param timeline_mode="live"
examples.description: Posts from @felixrieseberg between 2026-03-10 and 2026-03-18 on the Live tab.

returns: Array of post cards (author, text, posted_at, post_url, reply_count, repost_count, like_count, view_count) plus a search_url echo.
notes: Navigation is done via setTimeout + location.assign from the home page, which avoids the consent-overlay gotcha of typing into the landing search box.
Empty optional params are detected via the standard cleanArg helper.
timeline_mode=top uses the Top tab; latest and live both land on the Live tab.
